tickerText: [TENNIS] Queen's QF Friday: de Minaur vs Nakashima and home hope Arthur Fery vs Francisco Cerundolo headline eight quarter-finals on Andy Murray Arena — Day 4 saw qualifier Rinky Hijikata stun 2025 finalist Jiri Lehecka 4-6, 7-5, 7-6(7), 2024 champion Tommy Paul beat Botic van de Zandschulp 7-6(5), 6-3, and Davidovich Fokina topped Corentin Moutet 6-3, 6-4 • [BADMINTON] Macau Open quarter-final Friday at the East Asian Games Dome — the $370,000 Super 300 narrows to the last eight across all five disciplines with fourth seed Han Yue and a nine-strong Chinese contingent chasing weekend semi-finals • [SQUASH] PSA Tour Finals semi-finals LIVE in Paris: Orfi vs Sivasangari and El Hammamy vs Aboelkheir on the women's glass court; Asal vs Elias and Coll vs Zakaria in the men's semis at Centquatre — season-ending finals Saturday June 20 • [PICKLEBALL] Beijing Open QF day at China's National Tennis Center: quarter-finals across all divisions as PPA Tour Asia's $70,000 Olympic-venue debut reaches its business end ahead of Championship Sunday June 21 • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT Star Contender Ljubljana Day 4: Mizuki Oikawa shocks world No. 39 Jonathan Groth 3-2 in the R64; Manush Shah outlasts rising Chinese star Chen Junson in five; Kanak Jha (world No. 24) pushes through the main draw in Slovenia

newsItems.title: Paris Semi-Finals Set at PSA Tour Finals
newsItems.excerpt: The PSA Squash Tour Finals reach semi-final Friday at Centquatre-Paris, with world champion Amina Orfi facing Malaysia's Sivasangari and Hania El Hammamy taking on Fayrouz Aboelkheir — who stunned Nour ElSherbini in a five-game quarter-final upset on Day 2. On the men's side, Mostafa Asal meets Diego Elias and Paul Coll squares off with debutant Mohamad Zakaria for spots in Saturday's $710,000 season finale.

newsItems.title: Macau Open Down to the Last Eight
newsItems.excerpt: The Sands China Ltd. Macau Open Super 300 shifted into quarter-finals on June 19 at the East Asian Games Dome, with the $370,000 BWF World Tour field narrowed to the last eight across men's and women's singles, doubles, and mixed doubles. Fourth seed Han Yue leads a deep Chinese contingent pushing toward weekend semi-finals at the season's 16th World Tour stop.

newsItems.title: Beijing Open Reaches Quarter-Finals
newsItems.excerpt: Day 3 of the Capital Securities Beijing Open brought quarter-final action across all events at China's National Tennis Center — the 2008 Olympic venue hosting PPA Tour Asia for the first time. A 384-player field is competing for $70,000 in pro prize money and 500 tour points, with semifinals Saturday and Championship Sunday set for June 21 on the Capital Diamond Court.

newsItems.title: Ljubljana Main Draw Delivers Day 4 Upsets
newsItems.excerpt: WTT Star Contender Ljubljana produced headline results on Day 4 as Japan's Mizuki Oikawa stunned world No. 39 Jonathan Groth 3-2 in the men's singles round of 64 and India's Manush Shah outlasted rising Chinese star Chen Junson in five games. Kanak Jha, freshly ranked world No. 24 after his Skopje runner-up finish, continues his main-draw run in Slovenia through June 21.
